title: The Dyer's Hand
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: In 1954 Fez, the last master indigo dyer must decide whether to sell his family's ancient formulas to a Paris fashion house.
long_description:
The Dyer's Hand follows Idris ben Omar, a 68-year-old master indigo dyer in the Fez medina whose hands have been blue for fifty-three years. He is the last of his line — his son chose law over the vats, and the Jewish dyers who once shared the quarter have all departed. When a Paris fashion house sends a representative with an offer to buy his formulas, Idris must confront the ghosts of his craft and decide what a lifetime's knowledge is worth when no one is left to inherit it.

Through the return of his old friend Yacob Cohen — a Jewish dyer now dying of cancer in Marseille — and the quiet persistence of his young apprentice Driss, Idris learns that the craft is larger than any single dyer. The novel is a meditation on tradition, color, friendship across faiths, and what endures when the hands that built something are no longer there.
